panels.title: Racing games — things to investigate
panels.content:
## Game state and mode

Racing games tend to separate their modes clearly — there is usually a value that changes between the main menu, car selection, active race, and results screen. That value may be a standalone state index, part of a larger mode structure, or split across multiple flags. The demo flag, if one exists, may live inside this same state index rather than as its own address.

## Position and finish

Live race position and the position recorded at the finish line are worth treating as separate searches. Some games write the final result to a different address only at the moment the player crosses the line, and it may revert or become invalid shortly after.

## Timers

Timers in racing games can take many forms and all of them are worth investigating: a float value, a frame counter that increments once per frame, an integer stored in hex, a BCD value, centiseconds stored in a single address, or time split across separate addresses for minutes, seconds, and centiseconds. When searching, try increasing and decreasing scans during and between races and compare results across formats.

## Flags and bitmasks

Unlock states for cars, tracks, and bonus modes are often packed together. A single byte may represent several unlocks at once — if a value changes by 2 or 4 instead of 1, individual bits are worth inspecting. The same applies to per-race completion records and cheat states.

## Special mechanics

Values for nitrous, tricks, drafting, and similar mechanics may only be written to RAM while the relevant mode is active. If a search returns nothing, check that the game mode supports the mechanic before concluding the address does not exist.

panels.title: Protection
panels.infobox: Identify these before writing achievement logic. What qualifies as a demo, a valid save, or an active cheat varies by game — investigate each one.

entry_Protection.name: Demo mode
entry_Protection.tNote: Check for a specific value that is set or cleared only on the main menu or when demo mode initiates. This may be a dedicated flag, a value inside a larger game state index, or tied to content limits such as track count or lap cap.

entry_Protection.name: Save protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Find values that match a loading state or last loaded in-game state so that you can confirm the player is in-game for at least one frame by checking Delta = in-game. Consider what a brand-new or missing save looks like versus a valid loaded session.

entry_Protection.name: Cheat protection
entry_Protection.tNote: Investigate old guides or walkthroughs for possible cheats. If they offer an advantage, block them. Look for on/off flags, unnatural resource increases during a menu or pause scenario, or values that change outside of normal gameplay.

entry_Protection.name: Unlimited nitrous cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this cheat exists, nitrous-based achievements must require it to be off. Check what changes in RAM when it is active versus inactive.

entry_Protection.name: Invincibility / no damage cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this cheat exists, clean-race and collision-based achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Unlock all cars cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this cheat exists, car collection achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Unlock all tracks cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: If this cheat exists, track and progression achievements must require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: Speed modifier cheat
entry_Protection.tNote: Any cheat that alters base vehicle speed or handling beyond normal tuning options.

entry_Protection.name: Catch-up / rubberband assist
entry_Protection.tNote: Some games allow this to be toggled by the player. If so, challenge achievements may need to require it to be off.

entry_Protection.name: 2-player mode active
entry_Protection.tNote: A second human player changes race dynamics. Investigate whether this needs to be excluded from solo challenges.

panels.title: Progression
panels.infobox: Values that track forward movement through career, championship, or story structure.

entry_Progression.name: Game state / screen index
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes between the main menu, car select, active race, results screen, and any other major screens. May be a standalone index or part of a larger structure.

entry_Progression.name: Race mode ID
entry_Progression.tNote: What separates Championship from Single Race, Time Trial, Arcade, Practice, and any other named modes.

entry_Progression.name: Track / stage ID
entry_Progression.tNote: The identifier for whichever track is currently loaded or selected.

entry_Progression.name: Championship stage index
entry_Progression.tNote: Which race within a championship series the player is currently on.

entry_Progression.name: Difficulty setting
entry_Progression.tNote: The selected difficulty level. May be an index, an enum, or individual flags per difficulty tier.

entry_Progression.name: Race completion state
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes when a race ends, separate from position at the finish line.

entry_Progression.name: Championship completion state
entry_Progression.tNote: A value that changes when a full championship series is won.

entry_Progression.name: Easy championship cleared
entry_Progression.tNote: A flag or value indicating the easy championship has been completed. May unlock a bonus mode.

entry_Progression.name: Medium championship cleared
entry_Progression.tNote: A flag or value indicating the medium championship has been completed. May unlock a bonus mode.

entry_Progression.name: Hard championship cleared
entry_Progression.tNote: A flag or value indicating the hard championship has been completed. May unlock a bonus mode.

entry_Progression.name: Per-race event completion flags
entry_Progression.tNote: Individual flags or bits indicating which races in a championship have been finished. May be packed into one or more bytes.

entry_Progression.name: Mode unlock: Reverse
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether the Reverse mode bonus has been unlocked.

entry_Progression.name: Mode unlock: Pocket Bike
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether the Pocket Bike mode bonus has been unlocked.

entry_Progression.name: Mode unlock: Reverse Pocket
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether the Reverse Pocket mode bonus has been unlocked.

entry_Progression.name: Bonus mode unlock flags (combined)
entry_Progression.tNote: All bonus mode states may live in a single byte rather than separate addresses. Investigate both possibilities.

entry_Progression.name: Podium result
entry_Progression.tNote: Whether the player finished in a top-3 position. May be a separate flag from raw race position.

entry_Progression.name: DNF / disqualification flag
entry_Progression.tNote: A state that is set when a race ends in failure rather than completion.

entry_Progression.name: Checkpoint index
entry_Progression.tNote: Which checkpoint the player last passed. Changes as the player progresses through a race.

entry_Progression.name: Total career races completed
entry_Progression.tNote: A running count of finished races, if one exists.

entry_Progression.name: Region / season progress index
entry_Progression.tNote: For games with a world-tour or multi-region structure, which region or leg is currently active.

panels.title: Collections
panels.infobox: Things the player has unlocked or accumulated that carry across sessions.

entry_Collection.name: Car unlock state
entry_Collection.tNote: Which cars are available. May be individual flags, a bitmask, a count, or a combination.

entry_Collection.name: Garage slot 0 — car ID
entry_Collection.tNote: Which car is stored in the first garage slot. Additional slots may follow a repeating offset pattern.

entry_Collection.name: Garage slot 1 — car ID
entry_Collection.tNote: Second garage slot. Compare address offset from slot 0 to determine the slot entry size.

entry_Collection.name: Garage slot 2 — car ID
entry_Collection.tNote: Third slot. The same offset pattern from slot 1 should apply.

entry_Collection.name: Garage slot 3 — car ID
entry_Collection.tNote: Fourth slot. Continue the pattern for any additional slots.

entry_Collection.name: Track unlock state
entry_Collection.tNote: Which tracks are available. May be individual flags, a bitmask, a count, or a combination.

entry_Collection.name: Livery / color unlock state
entry_Collection.tNote: Whether alternate car colors or liveries have been unlocked. May not exist.

entry_Collection.name: Stat / upgrade: Acceleration
entry_Collection.tNote: Current value or tier of the acceleration stat.

entry_Collection.name: Stat / upgrade: Top Speed
entry_Collection.tNote: Current value or tier of the top speed stat.

entry_Collection.name: Stat / upgrade: Brakes
entry_Collection.tNote: Current value or tier of the brakes stat.

entry_Collection.name: Stat / upgrade: Grip
entry_Collection.tNote: Current value or tier of the grip stat.

entry_Collection.name: Career credits / currency
entry_Collection.tNote: Accumulated currency used for purchases. May not exist if the game has no shop.

entry_Collection.name: Medal / result per track
entry_Collection.tNote: Performance record for each individual track. May be a rating, a medal tier, or a best-finish position.

entry_Collection.name: Total medals count
entry_Collection.tNote: A running total of medals earned. May exist alongside per-track records.

entry_Collection.name: Championship reward car
entry_Collection.tNote: A car granted as a championship reward rather than through normal selection.

entry_Collection.name: Special edition car flag
entry_Collection.tNote: Vehicles unlocked through specific conditions separate from the main roster.

entry_Collection.name: Challenge / playlist completion flags
entry_Collection.tNote: Which challenge events or online playlists have been completed.

panels.title: Challenges
panels.infobox: Values needed to build achievements with pass/fail conditions.

entry_Challenge.name: Race timer
entry_Challenge.tNote: May be a float, a frame counter, an integer in hex, a BCD value, centiseconds in a single address, or split across separate addresses for minutes, seconds, and centiseconds. Search for an increasing value during a race and investigate all formats.

entry_Challenge.name: Lap timer
entry_Challenge.tNote: The timer for the current lap in progress. May share a format with the race timer or use a different one. Resets at the end of each lap.

entry_Challenge.name: Best lap time
entry_Challenge.tNote: A stored record of the fastest lap set this session. Updates when a faster lap is completed.

entry_Challenge.name: Best times array — track 0
entry_Challenge.tNote: Stored personal best for track 0. Additional tracks may follow a repeating offset pattern.

entry_Challenge.name: Best times array — track 1
entry_Challenge.tNote: Track 1. Compare address offset from track 0 to determine the entry size.

entry_Challenge.name: Best times array — track 2
entry_Challenge.tNote: Track 2. The same offset from track 1 should apply.

entry_Challenge.name: Best times array — track 3
entry_Challenge.tNote: Continue the pattern for each additional track.

entry_Challenge.name: Race position (live)
entry_Challenge.tNote: The player's current rank within the active race field.

entry_Challenge.name: Race position (at finish)
entry_Challenge.tNote: The position recorded when the player crosses the finish line. May be written to a different address from live position.

entry_Challenge.name: Car ID (currently driven)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which car the player is racing in during the current race.

entry_Challenge.name: Collision / crash flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that changes when the player collides with a wall, obstacle, or another vehicle.

entry_Challenge.name: Collision counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: A count of collisions during the current race. May exist alongside the flag or instead of it.

entry_Challenge.name: Damage state
entry_Challenge.tNote: The current damage level of the vehicle. May be a percentage, a tier value, or a multi-step enum.

entry_Challenge.name: Nitrous level (current)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Current nitrous charge. May deplete continuously or in steps.

entry_Challenge.name: Nitrous active flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that is set while nitrous is actively being used.

entry_Challenge.name: Nitrous max / capacity
entry_Challenge.tNote: Maximum nitrous capacity. May change with upgrades.

entry_Challenge.name: Trick ID (active)
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which trick the player is currently performing. Only populated while a trick is in progress.

entry_Challenge.name: Trick landed flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that changes when a trick is successfully completed.

entry_Challenge.name: Trick counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: A count of tricks completed during the current race or session.

entry_Challenge.name: Airtime flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that indicates the player's vehicle is currently airborne.

entry_Challenge.name: Airtime accumulator
entry_Challenge.tNote: Total air time accumulated during the race. May be in frames, centiseconds, or another unit.

entry_Challenge.name: Drafting / slipstream active
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that is set while the player is in another vehicle's slipstream.

entry_Challenge.name: Drafting accumulator
entry_Challenge.tNote: Total time or distance spent drafting during the race.

entry_Challenge.name: Slingshot / draft pass flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that fires when a slipstream boost is used to overtake another vehicle.

entry_Challenge.name: Wheelie state flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that is set while the player is performing a wheelie.

entry_Challenge.name: Wheelie accumulator
entry_Challenge.tNote: Total wheelie distance or time accumulated. May not exist.

entry_Challenge.name: Near miss counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: A count that increments when the player narrowly avoids a collision. May not exist.

entry_Challenge.name: Current speed
entry_Challenge.tNote: The player's current speed. The displayed value and the internal value may use different units or scales.

entry_Challenge.name: Lap counter
entry_Challenge.tNote: Which lap the player is currently on.

entry_Challenge.name: Reset token count
entry_Challenge.tNote: How many resets the player has remaining in the current race. May not exist in all games.

entry_Challenge.name: Opponent / cop engagement
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that changes when police or rival AI actively engage the player.

entry_Challenge.name: Dirty pass / contact flag
entry_Challenge.tNote: A value that fires when the player makes contact with another vehicle while passing.
